tools.description: Scan this device for locally available inference engines (container images and native binaries) and register newly found ones.

runtime.description: Runtime filter: 'auto' scans both container and native (default), 'container' scans only K3S/Docker images, 'native' scans only local binaries

tools.name: engine.scan

tools.description: List inference engines registered in the local database with names, types, runtime (container/native), and statuses.
tools.name: engine.list

tools.description: Import an engine container image from a local OCI tar file and register it (airgap use case).

path.description: Absolute path to the OCI tar file, e.g. '/data/images/vllm-cuda.tar'

tools.name: engine.import

tools.description: Get or set a persistent system configuration value. Omit value to read, provide value to write. Sensitive keys are masked.

key.description: Configuration key: api_key, llm.endpoint, llm.model, llm.api_key, llm.user_agent, llm.extra_params, central.endpoint, central.api_key, support.enabled, support.endpoint, support.invite_code, support.worker_code

value.description: Value to set. Omit this field to read the current value.

tools.name: system.config

tools.description: Check agent subsystem availability and routing: whether L3a (Go Agent) is healthy, which endpoint/model is selected, and what fallback candidates exist.
tools.name: agent.status

tools.description: Show full details of a deployment scenario including deployments, memory budget, startup order, alternative configs, integrations, verification results, and open questions.

name.description: Scenario name, e.g. 'openclaw-multi'. Call catalog.list with kind=scenarios to see available scenarios.

tools.name: scenario.show

tools.description: Infrastructure stack management. action=status: check installation status of stack components (K3S, HAMi) with versions and health. action=preflight: check which components need downloads before installation. action=init: install and configure the infrastructure stack. Blocked for agent-initiated calls (init only).

allow_download.description: Auto-download missing component files (for action=init, default false)

tools.name: stack

tools.description: Patrol loop management. action=status: get patrol loop state (running status, last run, next run, alert count). action=alerts: list active patrol alerts with severity and message. action=config: get or set patrol configuration (interval, thresholds, self_heal). action=actions: list automated actions taken by the patrol loop.

limit.description: Max actions to return (for action=actions, default 50)

tools.name: patrol

tools.description: Execute any MCP tool on a remote fleet device. Nested fleet.exec calls are blocked; when invoked by the Agent, the adapter applies the same inner-tool guardrails as local calls.

device_id.description: Device ID from fleet.info, e.g. 'gb10', 'linux-1'. Call fleet.info first if unsure.

tool_name.description: MCP tool name to execute remotely, e.g. 'hardware.detect', 'model.list', 'deploy.status'. Call fleet.info with device_id to see available tools.

params.description: Tool parameters as a JSON object. Omit or pass {} if the tool takes no parameters.

tools.name: fleet.exec

tools.description: Manage edge device onboarding (cold-start wizard) — used by both human UI and AI agents. Actions: start (read-only first-run guide), status (read-only), scan (read-only), recommend (read-only), init (destructive — installs docker/k3s stack), deploy (destructive — applies a deployment).

tier.description: Init action: which stack tier to install. Default: auto.

allow_download.description: Init action: allow internet download. Default: false.

engine.description: Deploy action: engine type (e.g. vllm, llamacpp). Optional — auto-resolved if empty.

config_overrides.description: Deploy action: override resolved config values. Optional.

no_pull.description: Deploy action: skip image pull. Default: false.

tools.name: onboarding

tools.description: Export knowledge data (configurations, benchmarks, notes) to JSON. Filter by hardware, model, or engine.

output_path.description: File path to write JSON. If omitted, returns JSON in response.

tools.name: data.export

tools.description: Show this edge's aima-service registration state: device_id, token expiry, registration_state (unregistered / pending / registered / failed), and referral code.
tools.name: device.status

tools.description: Fleet device information. Without device_id: list all AIMA devices on the LAN via mDNS. With device_id: get detailed information about a remote device (hardware, models, deployments) combined with its available MCP tools.

device_id.description: Device ID from fleet.info (no args), e.g. 'gb10', 'mac-m4'. Omit to list all devices.

tools.name: fleet.info

tools.description: Advanced knowledge analytics. query=compare: compare configs side-by-side. query=similar: find configs with similar performance profiles. query=lineage: trace derivation chain. query=gaps: identify untested combinations. query=aggregate: aggregate benchmark stats grouped by dimension.

config_id.description: Reference config ID (required for similar, lineage)

tools.name: knowledge.analytics

tools.description: Detect this device's hardware capabilities: GPU model, VRAM, compute SDK, CPU cores, total RAM, and NPU if present.
tools.name: hardware.detect

tools.description: Remove a model record from the database. Optionally deletes model files from disk. This is a destructive operation (a rollback snapshot is created automatically). Blocked for agent-initiated calls.

name.description: Model name to remove, e.g. 'qwen3-0.6b'. Call model.list to see registered models.

delete_files.description: If true, also delete model files from disk. If false (default), only removes the database record.

tools.name: model.remove

tools.description: Import knowledge data from a JSON file. Conflict resolution: 'skip' (default) or 'overwrite'. Supports dry-run. Atomic transaction.

input_path.description: Path to JSON file to import

tools.name: data.import

tools.description: Rollback snapshot management. action=list: list available rollback snapshots created before destructive operations. action=restore: restore a resource from a rollback snapshot (blocked for agent-initiated calls).

id.description: Snapshot ID from action=list (required for action=restore)

tools.name: agent.rollback

tools.description: Deploy all models defined in a deployment scenario. Supports dry_run to preview without executing.

name.description: Scenario name, e.g. 'openclaw-multi'. Call catalog.list with kind=scenarios to see available scenarios.

dry_run.description: If true, preview deployment plans without executing (default false)

tools.name: scenario.apply

tools.description: List models registered in the local database with names, file paths, sizes, and statuses.
tools.name: model.list

tools.description: Get detailed deployment state for one deployment: model, engine, slot, runtime, ready address, config, startup progress, and failure detail. Accepts deployment name or model name.

name.description: Deployment name (e.g. 'aima-vllm-qwen3-0-6b') or model name (e.g. 'qwen3-0.6b'). Call deploy.list if unsure.

tools.name: deploy.status

tools.description: Get a combined system overview: hardware summary, active deployments, and GPU metrics in one call.
tools.name: system.status

tools.description: Export a telemetry-free local diagnostics bundle for troubleshooting. Secrets are redacted. By default writes a JSON file under the local AIMA data directory; set inline=true to return the bundle directly.

output_path.description: Optional output JSON path. If omitted and inline=false, AIMA writes to its local diagnostics directory.

inline.description: Return the diagnostics bundle directly instead of writing a file. Default: false.

include_logs.description: Include redacted deployment log tails. Default: true.

log_lines.description: Deployment log tail lines per deployment. Default: 80.

tools.name: system.diagnostics

tools.description: Central server advisory. action=request: request an AI-powered engine/config recommendation for a model. action=feedback: send feedback on a received advisory after local validation.

model.description: Model name to get advice for, e.g. 'qwen3-8b' (for request)

engine.description: Engine type, e.g. 'vllm'. Omit to get a recommendation (for request).

advisory_id.description: Advisory ID from central server (for feedback)

status.description: Feedback status; validated is preferred (for feedback)

reason.description: Explanation of why advisory was validated or rejected (for feedback)

tools.name: central.advise

tools.description: Scan the local filesystem for model files (GGUF, SafeTensors) and register newly discovered ones in the database.
tools.name: model.scan

tools.description: Get recent log output from a deployment. Accepts deployment name or model name.

name.description: Deployment name (e.g. 'aima-vllm-qwen3-0-6b') or model name. Call deploy.list if unsure.

tail.description: Number of log lines to return, e.g. 50. Default: 100.

tools.name: deploy.logs

tools.description: Search knowledge records. scope=configs (default): search tested Configuration records with filtering and benchmark metrics. scope=notes: search knowledge notes by hardware/model/engine filter. scope=all: returns both.

scope.description: What to search: configs (default), notes, or all

tools.name: knowledge.search

tools.description: Knowledge evaluation actions. action=validate: compare predicted vs actual performance (flags >20% deviation). action=engine_switch_cost: quantify cost vs benefit of switching engines. action=open_questions: list, resolve, or launch exploration runs for open questions from knowledge assets.

target_engine.description: Engine to evaluate switching to (for engine_switch_cost)

status.description: Filter by question status (for open_questions list)

tools.name: knowledge.evaluate

tools.description: Route a natural language query through the Go Agent (L3a). Returns the agent's response and a session_id for multi-turn conversations. Blocked for agent-initiated calls (prevents recursive invocation).

dangerously_skip_permissions.description: Skip deploy approval gate (use with caution)

session_id.description: Session ID to continue a conversation

tools.name: agent.ask

tools.description: Deploy a model as an inference service. Auto-detects hardware, resolves optimal config, creates K3S Pod or native process. Returns NEEDS_APPROVAL — present the plan to the user, then call deploy.approve with the approval ID.

model.description: Model to deploy, e.g. 'qwen3-0.6b'. Call model.list to verify it is available locally.

engine.description: Engine type, e.g. 'vllm', 'llamacpp'. Omit to auto-select the best engine for this hardware.

slot.description: Partition slot for multi-model deployment, e.g. 'slot-0'. Omit for default full-device allocation.

max_cold_start_s.description: Maximum acceptable cold start time in seconds. Engines exceeding this are excluded from auto-selection. 0 or omitted means no constraint.

auto_pull.description: Whether to auto-download missing models/engine images. Defaults to true. Set false to fail fast if resources are not locally available.

no_pull.description: Alias for auto_pull=false. Require all model/engine assets to already exist locally.

tools.name: deploy.apply

tools.description: Auto-tuning session management. action=start: start an auto-tuning session (iterates config combos, benchmarks, promotes best). action=status: get session progress (configs tested, current best, ETA). action=stop: cancel the ongoing session (best config found so far is deployed). action=results: get results of the last completed session.

model.description: Model name to tune (required for start)

hardware.description: Hardware profile for benchmark persistence (for start)

engine.description: Engine type, auto-detect if empty (for start)

endpoint.description: Inference endpoint override for benchmarking (for start)

max_configs.description: Maximum configs to test, default 20 (for start)

tools.name: tuning

tools.description: Approve and execute a pending deployment. Call only after presenting the plan from deploy.apply to the user and receiving confirmation.

id.description: Approval ID from the deploy.apply NEEDS_APPROVAL response, e.g. 1

tools.name: deploy.approve

tools.description: Download a model by name from a remote source and register it in the database.

name.description: Model name to download, e.g. 'qwen3-0.6b', 'qwen3.5-35b-a3b'. Must match a name in the knowledge base (call catalog.list with kind=models to see available names).

tools.name: model.pull

tools.description: Get full information about a specific engine: availability, hardware requirements, startup config, supported features, and constraints.

name.description: Engine type (e.g. 'llamacpp', 'vllm', 'sglang'), image name, or engine ID. Call engine.list to see available names.

tools.name: engine.info

tools.description: Import a model from a local file path and register it in the database.

path.description: Absolute path to a model file (e.g. '/data/models/qwen3-0.6b.gguf') or directory containing model files

tools.name: model.import

tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific model: file path, size, format, quantization, and knowledge base metadata.

name.description: Model name as registered in the database, e.g. 'qwen3-0.6b'. Call model.list to see available names.

tools.name: model.info

tools.description: Download an inference engine image or binary from its configured source. Downloads a container image or native binary depending on this device's platform. If name is omitted, pulls the default engine for this hardware. Automatically registers the engine after pulling.

name.description: Engine type to pull, e.g. 'llamacpp', 'vllm', 'sglang'. Omit to pull the default engine for this hardware.

tools.name: engine.pull

tools.description: One-step: resolve config, pull engine and model if needed, deploy, and wait for the service to be ready. Combines deploy.dry_run + engine.pull + model.pull + deploy.apply + deploy.status polling. Returns when the service is ready or after timeout.

engine.description: Engine type override. Omit to auto-select.

slot.description: Partition slot name. Omit for default.

max_cold_start_s.description: Maximum acceptable cold start time in seconds. Engines exceeding this are excluded from auto-selection.

no_pull.description: Skip auto-downloading missing engine/model. Default false.

tools.name: deploy.run

tools.description: Find the optimal engine and configuration for deploying a model on this hardware. Merges YAML defaults, golden configs, and user overrides into a final resolved config.

model.description: Model name to resolve, e.g. 'qwen3-0.6b'. Call model.list or catalog.list to see available names.

engine.description: Engine type, e.g. 'vllm', 'llamacpp'. Omit to auto-select the best engine.

overrides.description: Config overrides to apply on top of resolved defaults, e.g. {"gpu_memory_utilization": 0.85}

tools.name: knowledge.resolve

tools.description: Record a benchmark result with performance metrics. Auto-creates a Configuration record if needed.

modality.description: Benchmark modality: llm (default), vlm, embedding, tts, asr, image_gen, video_gen

input_len_bucket.description: Input length category (e.g. short, medium, long)

ram_usage_mib.description: Host RAM usage in MiB during the run

power_draw_watts.description: GPU power draw in watts during the run

gpu_utilization_pct.description: GPU utilization percent during the run

cpu_usage_pct.description: Host CPU usage percent during the run

audio_throughput.description: [TTS] Audio seconds generated per wall second

asr_throughput.description: [ASR] Audio hours processed per wall hour

tools.name: benchmark.record

tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE: clear this edge's aima-service identity (device_id, token, recovery code). The next `device.register` call produces a new cloud identity. Requires confirm=true.
tools.name: device.reset

tools.description: Exploration run management. action=start: start a persisted exploration run (tune, validate, open_question). action=status: get current status with events and progress. action=stop: stop a running exploration run. action=result: get final or current result with events and summaries. action=list_runs: list historical runs (optional status/kind/limit filters) for the Web UI. action=get_run_detail: fetch a single run row by id. action=get_run_events: list events for a run. action=get_workspace_doc: read a whitelisted markdown document from the active Explorer workspace (plan.md, summary.md, device-profile.md, available-combos.md, knowledge-base.md, experiment-facts.md, index.md).

id.description: Exploration run ID (required for status, stop, result, get_run_detail, get_run_events)

kind.description: Exploration kind (for start; optional filter for list_runs)

source_ref.description: Optional source reference such as gap_id or alert_id (for start)

target.description: Exploration target with hardware, model, engine (for start)

search_space.description: Parameter grid as key -> candidate array (for start)

benchmark_profile.description: Benchmark profile with endpoint, concurrency, rounds (for start)

status.description: Status filter (for list_runs): planning, needs_approval, queued, running, completed, failed, blocked

limit.description: Max rows to return (for list_runs, default 50)

doc.description: Workspace document name (for get_workspace_doc). Whitelist: plan.md, summary.md, device-profile.md, available-combos.md, knowledge-base.md, experiment-facts.md, index.md

tools.name: explore

tools.description: Remove an engine record from the local database. Optionally deletes the actual container image or native binary. A rollback snapshot is created automatically. Blocked for agent-initiated calls.

name.description: Engine name or ID to remove. Call engine.list to see registered engines.

delete_files.description: Also delete the actual container image (docker rmi) or native binary file. Default false.

tools.name: engine.remove

tools.description: Preview a deployment without executing it. Returns resolved config, hardware fitness report, generated Pod YAML, and warnings. No side effects. Set output=pod_yaml to get only the generated Pod YAML manifest.

engine.description: Engine type, e.g. 'vllm', 'llamacpp'. Omit to auto-select.

slot.description: Partition slot for multi-model, e.g. 'slot-0'. Omit for default.

max_cold_start_s.description: Maximum acceptable cold start time in seconds. Engines exceeding this are excluded from auto-selection.

output.description: Output format: omit for full dry-run report, 'pod_yaml' for K3S Pod YAML manifest only

tools.name: deploy.dry_run

tools.description: Delete a running deployment (stops the inference service and removes the K3S Pod or native process). This is a destructive operation (a rollback snapshot is created automatically). Blocked for agent-initiated calls.

name.description: Deployment name to delete, e.g. 'aima-vllm-qwen3-0-6b'. Call deploy.list to see active deployments.

tools.name: deploy.delete

tools.description: Validate engine YAML catalog for schema issues: missing registries, baked-in proxy URLs, single-point-of-failure registries, and local-only distribution markers.
tools.name: catalog.validate

tools.description: Sync knowledge with the central server. action=push: push local knowledge to central. action=pull: pull new knowledge from central (includes advisories and scenarios). action=status: show sync status, connectivity, and last push/pull timestamps.
tools.name: central.sync

tools.description: Connect this AIMA instance to the support service (https://aimaserver.com) as a device, and optionally create a remote help task from a natural-language description.

description.description: Optional natural-language request to create a support task immediately

endpoint.description: Optional override for support.endpoint; persisted when provided

invite_code.description: Optional invite code for first-time registration; persisted when provided

worker_code.description: Optional worker enrollment code for first-time registration; persisted when provided

recovery_code.description: Optional saved recovery code used when refreshing an older registration

referral_code.description: Optional referral code for self-service registration

tools.name: support

tools.description: Save a knowledge note recording exploration results, experiment findings, or recommendations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the note

content.description: Full text content of the note (findings, observations, recommendations)

tools.name: knowledge.save

tools.description: Write a YAML asset to the runtime overlay catalog (~/.aima/catalog/). Overrides factory-embedded asset or adds new one.

content.description: Full YAML content of the asset

tools.name: catalog.override

tools.description: Central server scenarios. action=generate: request the central server to generate an AI-powered multi-model deployment scenario. action=list: list deployment scenarios from the central server filtered by hardware or source. action=feedback: report the outcome after applying a scenario so the central server can close the feedback loop.

hardware.description: Hardware profile, e.g. 'nvidia-gb10-arm64' (required for generate, optional filter for list)

models.description: Model names to include, e.g. ['qwen3-8b','glm-4.7-flash'] (required for generate)

tools.name: central.scenario

tools.description: Register this edge with aima-service (or re-register with a recovery code). The resulting device_id + token flow automatically to Central and all other cloud calls. Safe to call on an already-registered device; pass force=true to replace the existing identity.

invite_code.description: aima-service invite code; required for new devices

recovery_code.description: Saved recovery code; required when re-registering the same hardware

force.description: Re-register even if a valid token is already stored

tools.name: device.register

tools.description: Run a performance benchmark against a deployed model. Measures TTFT, TPOT, and throughput for LLM/VLM, or modality-specific metrics for TTS/ASR/T2I/T2V. Results auto-saved to database.

model.description: Model name (must match a deployed model)

endpoint.description: OpenAI-compatible endpoint URL. Auto-detected from proxy if omitted.

modality.description: Benchmark modality: llm (default), vlm, embedding, tts, asr, image_gen, video_gen

rounds.description: Number of measurement rounds (default: 1). Multiple rounds improve statistical significance.

min_output_ratio.description: Minimum output tokens as ratio of max_tokens (0-1, default: 0). Retries requests below this threshold.

max_retries.description: Per-request retry count on failure or output too short (default: 0)

save.description: Save results to knowledge DB (default: true)

hardware.description: Hardware profile ID for saving (e.g. nvidia-gb10-arm64)

deploy_config.description: Resolved engine startup config to persist as the Configuration record when saving results.

audio_files.description: [ASR] Audio file paths for transcription benchmark

tools.name: benchmark.run

tools.description: Run a benchmark matrix across multiple concurrency levels and input/output length combinations. Supports multi-modal sweep dimensions.

endpoint.description: OpenAI-compatible endpoint URL. Auto-detected from proxy if omitted.

min_output_ratio.description: Minimum output tokens ratio for retry (0-1, default: 0)

save.description: Save results to knowledge DB (default: true)

deploy_config.description: Resolved engine startup config to persist for every saved cell.

tools.name: benchmark.matrix

tools.description: List benchmark results from the database. Filter by model, hardware, modality, or configuration ID.
tools.name: benchmark.list

tools.description: List catalog assets. kind=profiles: hardware profiles with GPU/CPU/RAM vectors. kind=engines: engine assets with hardware requirements. kind=models: model assets with variants and sources. kind=partitions: partition strategies. kind=scenarios: deployment scenario recipes. kind=summary: counts of all asset types. kind=status: factory vs overlay asset counts. kind=all: all of the above combined.
tools.name: catalog.list

tools.description: Force-renew this edge's aima-service bearer token. Normally unnecessary — the background renewer handles this automatically.
tools.name: device.renew

tools.description: Collect real-time hardware metrics: GPU utilization, memory used/total, temperature, and power draw.
tools.name: hardware.metrics

tools.description: Autonomous Explorer management. action=status: show current state (tier, active plan, schedule config, last run). action=config: get or update Explorer schedule configuration. action=trigger: manually trigger an Explorer gap scan cycle. action=cleanup: stop all active deployments to free GPU memory before exploration (use when status shows blocked_by_deploys). action=db_deltas: count rows in configurations/benchmark_results/exploration_events since an optional ISO timestamp (for Web UI's DB-delta counters).

key.description: Config key: gap_scan_interval, sync_interval, full_audit_interval, quiet_start, quiet_end, max_concurrent_runs, enabled, mode (continuous/once/budget), max_rounds, max_tokens_per_day, max_cycles, max_tasks. rounds_used is read-only (for action=config, config_action=set)

since.description: Optional ISO-8601 timestamp lower bound (for action=db_deltas). Omit to return absolute totals.

tools.name: explorer

tools.description: OpenClaw integration management. action=sync: sync AIMA deployed models to OpenClaw config (categorizes by modality, writes providers, manages MCP server entry). action=status: inspect current OpenClaw integration state (gateway reachability, config presence, sync drift). action=claim: explicitly claim legacy OpenClaw config that already points at the local AIMA proxy.

dry_run.description: Preview changes without writing (for sync and claim, default false)

sections.description: Optional claim sections: llm, asr, vision, tts, image_gen. Default claims all detectable sections (for claim).

tools.name: openclaw

tools.description: List active deployment overviews on this device: name, model, engine, slot, runtime, phase/status, ready address, and startup summary. Use deploy.status for full per-deployment details.
tools.name: deploy.list

tools.description: Change a Configuration's status to 'experiment', 'golden' (auto-injected as L2 defaults), or 'archived'.
tools.name: knowledge.promote
